body: Probe report: benchmark-results/beadbench/reasoning-probe-20260423T034159Z.json. Arm agent-bragi-lmstudio-qwen36-35b-low used provider bragi (LM Studio), model qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, timeout 60s, max_tokens 48. All probes timed out: none, thinking_map_32, thinking_map_0, qwen_off, qwen_budget_32, qwen_template_off. This confirms the earlier 45s timeout observation and shows even explicit disable controls are not returning within 60s on the current Bragi endpoint.
summary: Bragi qwen3.6 timed out for every reasoning-control probe

body: Expanded baseline evidence on 2026-04-23: agent-vidar-omlx-qwen36-27b-low completed task helix-build-tracker-docs successfully with verifier pass in 688349 ms. Report: benchmark-results/beadbench/run-20260423T035107Z-3709288/report.json. In the same session, Bragi LM Studio qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b timed out for all 60s reasoning probes. This supports the research question: Vidar OMLX Qwen can complete execute-bead under Qwen controls, while Bragi LM Studio Qwen currently cannot even complete probe calls under the tested control shapes.
summary: Vidar OMLX low-effort smoke succeeds while Bragi probes time out

APPROVE
Implementation cleanly switches LM Studio to the Qwen wire format, introduces a `StrictThinkingModelMatch` capability to keep OMLX strict while letting LM Studio silently strip Qwen fields for non-Qwen models, and documents the Bragi GGUF template blocker with concrete server metadata. Unit tests cover disable-signal serialization, lenient non-Qwen stripping, and the strict OMLX reject path.
